subject: feat(scripts): generate-theme-meta.mjs — drift detection + auto-fix
body:
Closes out the last deferred pattern item from THEME_DARK_MODE. Script
verifies that every combo's metaColorLight/metaColorDark hex matches the
DaisyUI oklch definition, across the three places these values live:
  1. src/constants/themes.ts (THEME_COMBOS)
  2. index.html inline bootstrap COMBOS map
  3. index.html static <meta name="theme-color"> tags (first combo wins
     the pre-JS fallback role)

Modes:
  - `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs`  — verify, exit 1 on drift
  - `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs --write`  — rewrite corrected hex

Accessed via `npm run check-theme-meta` for the common case.

Implementation notes:
  - Reads DaisyUI's per-theme oklch values from node_modules/daisyui/
    theme/object.js (CJS default export).
  - oklch → hex conversion inlined (~30 lines, matches pattern doc
    reference code). No color-conversion dependency.
  - Convention: light combos use their light theme's --color-primary
    (vibrant branded status bar); dark combos use their dark theme's
    --color-base-100 (seamless match to page background).
  - Catalog parsed from source via stable regex anchored on the
    `id: '...'` field; we own both the script and the file format, so
    acceptable. Script errors loudly if the catalog structure changes
    and the regex no longer matches.
  - Tested end-to-end: introduce drift → verify fails with diff →
    --write restores → verify passes → byte-for-byte identical to the
    pre-drift state.

Not wired into `npm run build` — keeps build fast. Intended usage:
manual after adding a combo / swapping DaisyUI theme / bumping DaisyUI
version.

Tags: feature, tooling, theme, drift-detection
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pattern-compliance
Semver: minor
